---
title: "Attaclone-dedup"
subtitle: "A Zotero plugin for detecting and managing duplicate file attachments"
---

## What it does

Large Zotero libraries accumulate duplicate PDFs attached to the same parent item — usually because the same file was imported through multiple routes (browser connector, manual drop, snapshot). **Attaclone-dedup** compares attachments by content hash, automatically removes byte-identical *bare* copies, and queues the ambiguous cases (anything carrying annotations, notes, or tags) for a manual review dialog so you never lose annotation work without confirming.

## Highlights

- Content-hash matching (MD5), with filename + size fallback.
- Safe auto-resolution: only byte-identical, annotation-free duplicates are removed without asking.
- Manual review dialog with optional **annotation merging** from discarded PDFs to the kept PDF.
- Scan scopes: selected items, current collection, or entire library.
- Dry-run mode for safe rehearsals.

## Get started

- [Installation](vignette.qmd#installation)
- [Walkthrough](vignette.qmd)
- [Source code on GitHub](https://github.com/CTTIR/attaclone-dedup)
- [Issue tracker](https://github.com/CTTIR/attaclone-dedup/issues)

## Compatibility

Zotero 7 and later.

## Citation

See [How to cite this plugin](https://github.com/CTTIR/attaclone-dedup#how-to-cite-this-plugin) in the repository README.
